sections.title: A: Open Cloze
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: A boyfriend or girlfriend we love dumps us. How do we react? Most of us go through a period of crying our eyes (1) ......
humans are unique – perhaps with the exception of gorillas and elephants – in producing tears in response (2) ...... emotional stimuli.
We might feel (3) ...... crying when we hear sad news, or even cry (4) ...... happiness.
or even cry (4) ...... happiness.
Scientists are unsure what, if any, benefits there are from crying as it seems to have little or no immediate effect (5) ...... the situation that has produced the tears.

However, many people do say that they feel the benefits (6) ...... a good cry in that after it they are emotionally stronger, so it’s possible that crying does in some way help us to get (7) ...... difficult emotional situations.
so it’s possible that crying does in some way help us to get (7) ...... difficult emotional situations.
Expressions such as ‘there’s no point crying over (8) ...... milk’
and ‘(9) ...... and bear it’ in English reflect a culture that – at least historically – does not value adult crying.
If you cry ‘crocodile tears’, you are (10) ...... on an act – pretending to be upset when in fact you are not.

sections.title: B: Key Word Transformation
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and eight words, including the word given.

items.sentence:
That’s the impressionist the audience didn't like. **badly**
That’s the impressionist ……………………………… the audience.

He was arrested when they caught him driving a stolen vehicle. **act**
Being ……………………………… a stolen vehicle, he was arrested.
items.answer: caught in the act of driving

items.sentence:
Where we live now is nothing like Coventry city centre, where we used to live. **cry**
Where we live now is ……………………………… Coventry city centre, where we used to live.

The price of prescriptions will increase tomorrow. **effect**
The price of prescriptions will increase ……………………………… tomorrow.

I can’t understand why anyone would want to commit suicide, can you? **own**
I can’t understand why anyone would want to ……………………………… , can you?

You're going to enjoy tonight! **treat**
You're ……………………………… tonight!

Honestly, it was impossible not to laugh when Mrs Harrison slipped in the corridor. **face**
Honestly, ……………………………… was impossible when Mrs Harrison slipped in the corridor.

The parents of that girl are furious about her expulsion. **arms**
That’s the girl ……………………………… about her expulsion.
items.answer: whose parents are up in arms

sections.title: C: Word Formation
sections.instructions: Complete the sentences by changing the form of the word in capitals.

items.sentence: Don’t be so ……………………. . Can’t you see you’ve upset Guy? **SENSE**
Steve’s bought a …………………….. little gadget for tuning his guitar. **HAND**
I want to make sure all my …………………….. will be financially secure if I’m incapacitated in any way. **DEPEND**
The audience showed their …………………….. by booing and slowly clapping their hands. **APPROVE**
I don’t consider myself a political …………………….. , but I am interested in current affairs. **ACT**
At the start of the seminar, they gave us a number of handouts and a nice little …………………………………… to keep them all in. **FOLD**
There’s a risk factor with all medication, but honestly the risk with this particular drug is ………………………………………… **NEGLECT**
We still don’t know whether the plan will ever come to ……………………………………… or not. **FRUIT**

sections.title: D: Phrasal Verbs / Expressions
sections.instructions: Write a verb from the box in the correct form in each gap. There is one verb you will not need.

items.sentence: I thought Tim was serious at first, but then I ………………………………… on to the fact that he was pulling Graham's leg.
I’ve …………………………………………… out in a rash all over my body.
I almost …………………………………………… up when I heard Doug was going to be playing Romeo in the school play.
I'm afraid Mr Jenkins …………………………………………… away in his sleep last night.
He's in a critical condition in hospital, so we're all praying he'll …………………………… through.
I started to feel dizzy and then completely …………………………………… out.
Did people really think that if you hung garlic by your bed it would ……………………… off vampires?
My grandad's old war wound is …………………………………………… up again, he says.
I got totally fed up with telesales, ...... being quite good at it.
...... they get the letter tomorrow, you still won’t get a reply before the weekend.
The woman ...... is being held at Granchester Police Station.
Britain has been in the European Community for more than 30 years. ...... , there are still occasional calls for its withdrawal.
...... I’d like to help you out, I’m afraid I just haven’t got any spare money at the moment.
Is that the bookshop ...... they’ve also got a café?
You’re the first person I’ve met ...... political views are more left-wing than mine!
This government would destroy all our civil liberties, ...... half a chance.
